assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address.
No Wise business account, personal API token, partner client credentials, or Developer Hub access exists yet.
Sandbox work is acceptable when the partner route is selected.

developer_goal: Create the required Wise access relationship and complete the earliest official first API success for that relationship.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved between personal-token profile listing for an SMB business account and partner OAuth first calls after sales-issued credentials.
first_success.observable_signal: Personal-token docs show GET /v1/profiles returning profiles, while partner guides require client credentials from Wise before sandbox API work.

route_selection.surface: Wise Getting started guide plus personal-token or Developer Hub auth routes
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after the access relationship (SMB personal token versus partner OAuth) is fixed; do not invent a winner across unequal entitlements.

candidates.name: Wise Business personal API token then GET profiles
candidates.reason: Official docs support self-serve token creation for automating an existing Wise business account, but this is a different access relationship from partner Platform integrations and has limited endpoints.

candidates.name: Partner or enterprise OAuth via Developer Hub
candidates.reason: Getting started states Wise issues client ID and secret during partner onboarding and must grant Developer Hub access, so self-serve shortest-path comparison is blocked.

candidates.name: Sandbox account for exploratory API play
candidates.reason: Help Centre describes a free sandbox signup path, but Platform getting-started still centers partner credentials and personal-token business automation as distinct models.

required_path.action: Establish the Wise account or partner relationship required by the selected access model.

required_fields.label: Access relationship choice
required_fields.notes: Personal token versus partner OAuth changes actors, credentials, and available endpoints.

required_fields.label: Account or partner onboarding fields
required_fields.notes: Public docs do not inventory current Wise.com business signup or sandbox signup fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has either a Wise business account or partner sandbox credentials path.

required_path.action: Create the credentials for the selected auth model.

required_fields.label: Personal API token or partner client credentials
required_fields.notes: Personal tokens require 2-step verification; partner credentials are issued by Wise.

required_path.observable_result: An API credential is available for authenticated requests.

required_path.action: Call the first documented authenticated endpoint for the selected route.

required_fields.label: First API operation
required_fields.notes: Personal-token example uses GET /v1/profiles while profile reference also documents /v2/profiles.

required_path.observable_result: The selected route returns a successful authenticated API response.

prerequisites.description: Personal API tokens require a Wise.com business account and 2-step verification.
Partner Developer Hub access and client credentials require Wise onboarding.

external_gates.description: Partner client credentials and Developer Hub login are granted by Wise, not created fully self-serve.
Personal tokens are limited to a documented subset of payout automation endpoints.

unavoidable_waits.description: Partner onboarding and implementation-team access may delay Developer Hub and credential availability.

platform_outcomes.description: Wise returns profile data for an authenticated personal-token GET profiles call.

excluded.item: Reading the full API reference and error guides
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

excluded.item: Funding transfers and balance-statement automation beyond personal-token limits
excluded.reason: Personal-token docs exclude most funding and statement operations except listed regions.
Help Centre places going live after sandbox or token experimentation.

sources.title: Getting started with the API (Help Centre)

uncertainties.question: Which Wise access relationship should represent Platform first success: SMB personal token or partner OAuth?
uncertainties.impact: The routes have different entitlement gates, credential creators, endpoint coverage, and whether self-serve account creation alone is enough.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy choice of one access model, then a fresh walkthrough of that model's account creation through first successful API response.

uncertainties.question: If the personal-token route is selected, which profiles operation version is current first success?
uncertainties.impact: The personal-token guide still shows GET /v1/profiles while profile reference also documents /v2/profiles.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Confirm the currently recommended profiles list operation against official docs and a live token response.
